Politics.headline: As states scrap for congressional seats, Virginia could tip the scales
Politics.detail: Virginia's well-funded canvassing and advertising campaign for a referendum could add four blue seats to Congress, leading Republicans to scramble in response. The referendum aims to redraw congressional districts, potentially shifting the balance of power.

Politics.headline: The Trump Administration's “Disturbing” New Legal Strategy to Prosecute Border Crossers Is Taxing Courts and Testing the Law
Politics.detail: The Justice Department is pursuing cases against individuals who admit to entering the U.S. illegally, even when ready for deportation, leading to lengthy jail times over unfounded allegations, puzzling legal experts. One man sat in jail for 40 days over allegations of trespassing on military land.

Politics.headline: UK 'will not be drawn into wider war' over Iran, says Starmer as he announces £53m to help with heating oil costs – UK politics live
Politics.detail: Keir Starmer stated that the UK will not be drawn into a wider war in the Middle East and announced £53m in aid to help with heating oil costs. This comes amidst ongoing conflict involving Iran and concerns about global stability.

Politics.headline: Trump's mass deportation agenda is at a crossroads with the Homeland Security shake-up
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump's decision to replace the Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem presents an opportunity to either reset his mass deportation agenda or to double down on his campaign promise. The White House is considering focusing on deporting criminals instead of mass deportations.

Politics.headline: Uzbekistan Improves Gas Network to Benefit Households and Businesses
Politics.detail: The World Bank approved a $10.6 million grant to help Uzbekistan reduce leaks in its gas transmission system. The project aims to recover valuable gas, boost energy security, increase state revenues, and create jobs.

Technology.headline: UK splashes £45M on AI supercomputer to help crack fusion power
Technology.detail: The 'Sunrise' supercomputer will run AI-heavy simulations of plasma behavior and reactor physics.

Technology.headline: Microsoft points at Samsung after Galaxy app bug locks users out of C:/
Technology.detail: 'Access denied' errors hit certain Windows 11 machines running vendor utility; Microsoft suggests a Samsung app is responsible.

Technology.headline: Horizon redress still a mess, MPs say – and Fujitsu hasn't paid a penny
Technology.detail: The system compensating victims of the Post Office Horizon scandal is still slow, with thousands of ex-subpostmasters waiting for payments.

Technology.headline: West Sussex's Oracle rollout pushed back again as costs balloon 15 times
Technology.detail: Already five years late, the project is delayed another six months after the price tag swells from £2.6M to £41M.

Technology.headline: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra: its huge screen blocks shoulder surfers from spying on you
Technology.detail: The Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra has a huge screen that makes it difficult for others to see what you are doing.

Health.headline: Just 24 Minutes of Specially Designed Music Could Significantly Reduce Anxiety
Health.detail: A clinical trial found that listening to specially designed music with auditory beat stimulation can significantly reduce anxiety, with a 24-minute session proving effective.

Health.headline: Scientists Create a Cancer Flashlight That Lights Up Tumors
Health.detail: Researchers have developed a tiny antibody that can find a common cancer protein and make tumors light up during PET scans, potentially helping doctors with diagnosis and treatment. In tests with mice, tumors containing the protein EphA2 glowed clearly when the antibody was used.

Health.headline: Anxiety and Depression May Raise Risk for Aneurysm Rupture
Health.detail: New findings suggest a potential link between anxiety and depression and an increased risk of aneurysm rupture.

Health.headline: The Alzheimer's gut-brain link: How butyrate could curb amyloid-β buildup and inflammation
Health.detail: Research explores how butyrate, a short-chain fatty acid produced in the gut, could potentially reduce amyloid-beta buildup and inflammation in the brain, offering a new avenue for Alzheimer's disease research.

Health.headline: Restraining and sedating dementia patients 'routine' in hospitals in England, study finds
Health.detail: A study reveals that the practice of restraining and sedating dementia patients is a common occurrence in hospitals in England, raising concerns about patient care and safety.

Sports.headline: Aaron Judge proclaims World Baseball Classic has surpassed World Series in importance
Sports.detail: Speaking after the U.S. beat the Dominican Republic 2-1 in the semifinal, Aaron Judge stated that the World Baseball Classic has become baseball's biggest stage, even bigger and better than the World Series.

Sports.headline: U.S. beats Dominican Republic 2-1 to reach 3rd straight WBC title game
Sports.detail: The United States defeated the Dominican Republic 2-1 to advance to their third consecutive World Baseball Classic title game. Gunnar Henderson homered in the fourth inning.

Sports.headline: Denny Hamlin storms back to win at Las Vegas after early penalty
Sports.detail: Denny Hamlin recovered from an early penalty to win at Las Vegas in an auto racing event.

Sports.headline: Senior player Kidambi & youngsters Ayush & Tanvi to lead India's challenge at Orleans Masters Super 300 tournament
Sports.detail: Kidambi, along with Ayush and Tanvi, will lead India's challenge at the Orleans Masters Super 300 tournament.

Sports.headline: Jannik Sinner beats Daniil Medvedev to win BNP Paribas Open in Indian Wells
Sports.detail: Jannik Sinner defeated Daniil Medvedev to win the BNP Paribas Open in Indian Wells.

Entertainment.headline: 'One Battle After Another' triumphs at 98th Academy Awards in coronation for Paul Thomas Anderson
Entertainment.detail: Paul Thomas Anderson's 'One Battle After Another' has been crowned best picture at the 98th Academy Awards, with Jessie Buckley winning best actress and Michael B. Jordan best actor.

Entertainment.headline: Michael B. Jordan wins best actor for 'Sinners,' taking home his first Oscar in popular victory
Entertainment.detail: Michael B. Jordan won the Oscar for best actor for his performance in 'Sinners'.

Entertainment.headline: Oscars 2026: One Battle after Another wins best picture. Golden makes award history with first K-pop best original song win.
Entertainment.detail: At the 2026 Oscars, 'One Battle After Another' won best picture, and Golden made award history by winning best original song, the first K-pop song to do so.

Entertainment.headline: Every Single Thing That Happened at the 2026 Oscars
Entertainment.detail: Michael B. Jordan wins Best Actor for Sinners, but Best Picture goes to One Battle After Another.

Entertainment.headline: Ireland celebrates Jessie Buckley as the country's first Best Actress Oscar winner: 'A historic moment'
Entertainment.detail: Ireland is celebrating Jessie Buckley as the country's first Best Actress Oscar winner.

Crime & Justice.headline: Closing arguments in Kouri Richins trial
Crime & Justice.detail: Closing arguments are expected to begin on Monday in Kouri Richins' murder trial, where she's accused of giving her husband a deadly dose of fentanyl four years ago before publishing a children's book about grief. If convicted, Richins could face life in prison.

Crime & Justice.headline: FBI: Synagogue attacker died by suicide
Crime & Justice.detail: The suspect in the Michigan synagogue attack died from a self-inflicted gunshot wound, the FBI said at a news conference on Friday. Authorities say 41-year-old Ayman Mohamad Ghazali waited in his car outside a Detroit-area synagogue for two hours before ramming into the building, and the FBI says he fatally shot himself.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ohio Cops Investigate Murder of 19-Year-Old Man Found Dead on Side of Road
Crime & Justice.detail: Police are investigating the murder of a 19-year-old man who was found dead on the side of the road. The circumstances surrounding his death are currently under investigation.

Crime & Justice.headline: New York lawyer linked to Trump pardon charged with attempted extortion
Crime & Justice.detail: Joshua Nass, a New York lawyer linked to a Trump pardon, has been charged with an alleged $600,000 extortion plot, and he played a role in the pardon of a man convicted of failing to pay $40m in taxes. The Justice Department is pursuing the case.

Crime & Justice.headline: Israel claims brother of Michigan synagogue attacker was Hezbollah commander
Crime & Justice.detail: The Israeli military claims on social media that the brother of Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, the Michigan synagogue attacker, was a Hezbollah commander who was 'eliminated in airstrike last week'. This connection adds a new dimension to the investigation of the attack.

Education.headline: Punjab State Teacher Eligibility Test 2026: Provisional Answer Key Out, Download Here
Education.detail: The provisional answer key for the Punjab State Teacher Eligibility Test 2026 has been released and is available for download.

Education.headline: Bihar Board Class 12 Result 2026: BSEB Inter Results Likely To Be Announced
Education.detail: The Bihar School Examination Board (BSEB) is likely to announce the Class 12 results for 2026 soon.

Education.headline: Education ministry aims for stricter curbs against teacher voyeurism
Education.detail: The Japanese education ministry is drafting revisions to guidelines to prevent sexual misconduct by teachers, including stronger measures against voyeurism, prompted by a recent incident involving teachers sharing secret videos of female students. The revisions will also clarify that teachers committing indecent acts against children should be dismissed.

Education.headline: Four education bills we're keeping an eye on as Colorado legislature reaches halfway point
Education.detail: Colorado legislators are debating education bills addressing affordable housing for teachers, protecting the civil rights of students with disabilities, and school funding.  Senate Bill 125 would reinforce the rights of kids with disabilities to ensure they have equal access to education and prevent discrimination.

Education.headline: Montgomery school board talks vacant roles, special ed, vaping
Education.detail: The Montgomery school board discussed staff vacancies, special education funding, and vaping. A report recommended expanding the Discovery program for gifted students, offering activities twice a month to all first-graders.

Lifestyle.headline: Japan's cherry blossom season begins with first flowering confirmed in 3 cities
Lifestyle.detail: The cherry blossom season in Japan has officially started, with the first flowering confirmed in three cities, signaling the beginning of spring and a time of celebration. The blooming of cherry blossoms, known as sakura, is a significant cultural event in Japan, attracting tourists and locals alike.

Lifestyle.headline: Oscars 2026 red carpet: Jessie Buckley, Chase Infiniti and more – in pictures
Lifestyle.detail: The 98th annual Academy Awards took place, showcasing stars decked out in their finest attire for Hollywood's biggest night. Photos from the event highlight the fashion and glamour of the red carpet.

Lifestyle.headline: Stars deliver modern and colorful looks on Oscars red carpet
Lifestyle.detail: Celebrities made bold fashion statements at the Oscars, paying close attention to their red-carpet attire. The event was the final stop on Hollywood's monthslong awards season.

Lifestyle.headline: Inside Chanel and Charles Finch's glamorous pre-Oscars dinner at the Polo Lounge
Lifestyle.detail: A glamorous pre-Oscars dinner hosted by Chanel and Charles Finch took place at the Polo Lounge. The event brought together celebrities and industry insiders ahead of the Academy Awards.

Lifestyle.headline: Three ex-Horses chefs have created L.A.'s true pop-up of the moment.
Lifestyle.detail: Three former chefs from Horses have launched a pop-up restaurant in Los Angeles. Food critic Bill Addison recommends the Bruce pop-up, which is currently located at Justine's Wine Bar in Frogtown.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Efficient water use means doing more with less.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses the importance of efficient water use and strategies for achieving it, emphasizing the need to maximize output while minimizing water consumption.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Gaza could be trapped in a 'forever' war
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial likely discusses the ongoing conflict in Gaza and the challenges in achieving a lasting resolution, suggesting a pessimistic outlook on the region's future.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Get serious, Hegseth
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial criticizes Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth for prioritizing his image over serious matters, especially during a war that impacts the global economy, and praises the uniformed military officers for their professionalism.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Hiking the minimum wage puts robots over people.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial board argues against raising the minimum wage, suggesting it would incentivize companies to replace human workers with automation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: No break looms for House impeachment hearings
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article discusses the impeachment complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte and suggests that the House of Representatives proceedings will likely continue beyond the March 18 recess.

World.headline: Iran's FM says Strait of Hormuz 'closed to our enemies'
World.detail: Amidst the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran, Iran's Foreign Minister declared that the Strait of Hormuz is closed to its enemies, raising concerns about global shipping and oil supplies. This statement follows reports of missile impacts near Jerusalem and explosions in the region.

World.headline: Drone attack disrupts Dubai flights as Iran continues Gulf attacks
World.detail: A drone attack caused a fire near Dubai International Airport, leading to temporary suspension of flights as tensions remain high in the region due to ongoing attacks attributed to Iran. This is happening as the war on Iran enters its third week.

World.headline: 'Utter disregard for Palestinian lives': Israeli soldiers kill parents, two young boys
World.detail: In the West Bank, Israeli soldiers opened fire on a car, killing a Palestinian couple and their two young children, aged 5 and 7, sparking outrage and condemnation. This incident highlights the continuing violence and tensions in the region.

World.headline: EU, UN discussing Black Sea type initiative for Strait of Hormuz
World.detail: The EU and UN are reportedly discussing a Black Sea-type initiative to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, which is vital for global oil supplies, amidst the ongoing conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran. This comes as Trump demands other countries help secure the Strait.

World.headline: Videos capture moment hot air balloon knocks kayakers into lake
World.detail: During the Canberra Balloon Spectacular, a hot air balloon unexpectedly collided with kayakers on Lake Burley Griffin, sending them into the water. Fortunately, no serious injuries were reported.

Zimbabwe.headline: Rusape Robbers Arrested After Dropping Gun and Cellphones at Crime Scene
Zimbabwe.detail: Police in Rusape have arrested a group of robbers after they dropped their gun and cellphones at the crime scene, leading to their swift apprehension.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe Set to Commission Four Lithium Processing Plants by Year End
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is on track to commission four lithium processing plants by the end of the year, signaling progress in the country's efforts to benefit from its lithium resources.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mutsvangwa calls for diplomatic solution to Middle East conflict
Zimbabwe.detail: ZANU PF spokesperson, Christopher Mutsvangwa, has called for a diplomatic resolution to the ongoing Middle East conflict, blaming the unrest on the denial of Palestinian rights.

Zimbabwe.headline: Apostolic sect backs Mnangagwa's term extension
Zimbabwe.detail: About 10 000 congregants of Johanne The 5th of Africa International brought business to a standstill at Birchenough Bridge Centre as marched in support of Constitutional Amendment No 3.

Zimbabwe.headline: Barbara Rwodzi Named Zimbabwe's Best Performing Minister At 2026 Performance Awards
Zimbabwe.detail: Tourism and Hospitality Industry Minister Barbara Rwodzi has been ranked as the top-performing minister in Zimbabwe's latest government evaluation, securing first place ahead of 24 cabinet colleagues at a performance review ceremony held this morning at the Harare International Conference Centre.
